results.title: Plis Chat Me | LinkedIn
excerpts:
Section Title: Plis Chat Me > About us
Content:
Plis Chat Me es una solución SaaS para empresas que buscan centralizar y optimizar sus comunicaciones multicanal. Desarrollado por Sentinel México, esta herramienta permite conectar plataformas como WhatsApp, Telegram, Instagram, Facebook Messenger y más en una única interfaz. Con un CRM integrado, facilita la gestión de clientes, la creación de tickets de soporte y el desarrollo de bases de conocimiento. Diseñada para empresas de todos los tamaños, Plis Chat Me mejora la eficiencia operativa y la experiencia del cliente, eliminando barreras lingüísticas y geográficas. Su enfoque en innovación, confianza y simplicidad la convierte en una herramienta esencial para la transformación digital.

results.title: Adding intercom conversations into hubspot | Community
excerpts:
Section Title: Adding intercom conversations into hubspot > 7 replies
Content:
As others in this thread have experienced, the native integration creates multiple timeline entries per conversation (8+ as ​Chris showed), transcripts get messy for longer chats, and there's no way to sync tickets to HubSpot.
I built Interhubz (disclaimer: it's my app, available in both the Intercom and HubSpot marketplaces) specifically to solve these problems. Here's the short version of how it works:
**One note per conversation** : Interhubz waits until the conversation is closed (works with other triggers too), then creates a single formatted note on the HubSpot contact with the full transcript, attachments, tags, and a link back to Intercom. No drip-syncing, no timeline spam.
**Smart matching** : It matches conversations to existing HubSpot contacts by email, phone, or custom properties before creating anything, so no duplicates.
 ...

Content:
By Microsoft
4.5
[(1604Ratings)](https://www.gartner.com/reviews/product/microsoft-teams?marketSeoName=unified-communications-as-a-service)
OVERVIEW ALTERNATIVES
Microsoft Teams is a collaboration software that integrates chat, video meetings, file storage and application integration to facilitate communication and teamwork. It supports real-time messaging, voice and video conferencing, and threaded conversations, enabling users to collaborate on projects and share files within team channels. The software allows integration with various third-party and Microsoft 365 applications, providing a centralized workspace for document management and workflow automation. Microsoft Teams is designed to support remote and distributed work environments, addressing business needs for secure communication, coordination, and productivity among organizations of varying sizes.
Show More Details
 ... 
Section Title: Unified Communications as a Service Reviews and Ratings > ... > Mandatory Features:
Content:
Messaging features: The following capabilities must be included, at a minimum:
1. The ability to enable users to exchange richly formatted instant messages (IMs) with emojis, images, file attachments and links in real time. 2. Support for personal messaging for 1:1 communication (also known as “chat”), as well as team messaging for groups using conversational user experiences. 3. Support for presence and status indicators that allows users to view the availability and status of other users and resources in their organizations. Enterprise telephony features: This functionality must be supported through physical IP phones, physical analog phones; analog devices (e.g., fax machines and fire alarm panels); desktop apps; web apps; or mobile apps. It must include at least the following features:
1. Emergency calling services with notifications connectivity to the PSTN. 2. Survivability
3.

results.title: The Best CRM SaaS Software: Guide & Reviews
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > [Hubspot CRM](https://www.hubspot.com/products/crm)
Content:
[Image credit](https://www.hubspot.com/hubfs/assets/hubspot.com/homepage/evergreen-homepage/Hero%20Image%20-%20Desktop.jpg)
Hubspot’s CRM solution is specifically built to grow with the businesses they serve. They offer a free trial, as well as a free version with up to 1 million contacts and no expiration date.
Detailed tracking and quota management tools keep users up-to-date on their team’s performance. HubSpot also offers free email templates and scheduling software to help sales reps devote more time to customer-centric activities. Plus, with its sales pipeline management tools, representatives can get their bearings on a lead’s status whenever they interact with them.
Features:
Email tracking and notifications
Click-to-call functionality
Prospect tracking
Lead segmentation
Business card scanner app

results.title: 12 Best CRM for SaaS Companies to Drive Growth - Lark
excerpts:
Section Title: 12 Best SaaS CRM Tools to Drive Retention & Guide Included
Content:
[Lark Messenger](https://www.larksuite.com/en_us/product/messenger) supports direct and group chats with file sharing, document previews, and alerts. For SaaS teams, conversations about customer accounts, renewals, or onboarding can happen alongside the related [Lark Base records](https://www.larksuite.com/hc/en-US/articles/800678647524-quick-add-new-base-records) or [Approval requests](https://www.larksuite.com/hc/en-US/articles/834786153503-get-started-with-requesting-approval) , keeping discussions contextual and traceable. ​

Lark Calendar and Lark Meetings: Scheduling renewals and customer calls ​

Section Title: 12 Best SaaS CRM Tools to Drive Retention & Guide Included
Content:
All-in-one workspace to manage the entire SaaS customer lifecycle in one place. ​
Track subscriptions, renewals, and customer health with real-time dashboards—no tool-switching required. ​
Automated workflows handle reminders, onboarding tasks, and renewal follow-ups, so nothing slips through. ​
Built-in Messenger, Docs, and Calendar keep sales, success, and product teams aligned. ​
Mobile-friendly access means you can monitor accounts, approve renewals, and chat with teammates anytime, anywhere. ​
​ ### 2. HubSpot CRM: Best for SaaS startups ​ ​

Section Title: 12 Best SaaS CRM Tools to Drive Retention & Guide Included
Content:
| Feature / Need ​ | Traditional CRMs ​ | Spreadsheets (Excel/Sheets) ​ | Lark ​ |
| Subscription tracking ​ | Limited pipeline views ​ | Manual, error-prone ​ | Custom views in Lark Base with real-time updates ​ |
| Renewal & churn prevention ​ | Requires add-ons or reports ​ | Hard to track ​ | Dashboards highlight at-risk accounts instantly ​ |
| Onboarding workflows ​ | Manual task setup ​ | Scattered across sheets ​ | Tasks + Approval automate steps & approvals ​ |
| Knowledge sharing ​ | Often separate wiki tool ​ | Files easily lost ​ | Docs + Wiki = co-editing + searchable playbooks ​ |
| Cross-team collaboration ​ | Add-ons for chat & meetings ​ | Outside tools needed ​ | Messenger + Meetings built in, context-rich ​ |
| Mobile-friendly access ​ | Limited app experience ​ | Basic editing only ​ | Full mobile access with workflows, chat & dashboards ​ |

results.title: Crm Vs Saas - Difference & Relationship between the two - Smartagentic Website
excerpts:
Section Title: Saas Vs Crm – > **SaaS (Software as a Service)** > Characteristics of Saas –
Content:
**Cloud-Based :**
SaaS solutions are hosted centrally on a supplier’s servers and users access these solutions through the web.
**Subscription Model :**
In most cases you subscribe to it and for this you are required to make regular payments of either an amount per month or per year.
**No Installation Required :**
You don’t install anything on your computer, you simply log in through your web browser.
**Automatic Updates** :
Some of the benefits include: the provider managing all the software updates and there is always the latest version of the software.
**Accessibility :**
One of the main advantages of such software is that you can get at it from any place with an internet connection.
Examples of SaaS applications include:
**Email marketing platforms** **:** Mailchimp, Constant Contact
**Project management tools** **:** Asana, Trello
**File storage and sharing** **:** Google Drive, Dropbox
 ... 
Section Title: Saas Vs Crm – > Difference between Saas and CRM –
Content:
| Aspect | SaaS (Software as a Service) | CRM (Customer Relationship Management) |
| **Meaning** | A method of delivering software over the internet without requiring installation. | A tool designed to manage and enhance interactions with customers or clients. |
| **Function** | Offers a variety of online applications for different business needs. | Focuses specifically on managing customer relationships and communication. |
| **Usage** | Can be used for multiple purposes like storage, project management, and marketing. | Primarily used to track leads, automate communication, **[Pipeline Management Implementation](https://www.smartagentic.com/blog/importance-and-implementation-of-pipeline-management/)** and improve customer engagement. |
| **Examples** | Dropbox, Trello, Google Drive. | Zoho CRM, HubSpot, Salesforce. |

results.title: Best Unified API Platforms for Developers and SaaS Teams
excerpts:
Section Title: Best Unified API Platforms for Developers and SaaS Teams
Content:
Trying to build a product while maintaining dozens of integrations and keeping up with the constant churn of third-party APIs is a recipe for burnout and falling behind on customer requests. You can get stuck in a cycle of ad hoc builds, weekly API changes, and mounting support tickets. If this sounds familiar, you should consider a Unified API to transform your approach.
A unified API abstracts multiple third-party APIs in a particular category (like HR, CRM, or accounting) into a single normalized API. Instead of learning Salesforce's REST patterns, HubSpot's quirky authentication, and QuickBooks' XML responses, you build against one consistent interface.
Here's why this matters more than you might think:
 ...
